author: y4rr
content:
Plan: C9.3 — ferrotorch-nn structural+recurrent+extension conformance suite.

Scope: 10 modules:
1. container.rs  — Sequential, ModuleList, ModuleDict
2. module.rs     — Module trait / state_dict / load_state_dict / children / named_modules
3. parameter.rs  — Parameter (new, zeros, ones, from_slice, to, set_data, set_requires_grad)
4. parameter_container.rs — ParameterList, ParameterDict
5. buffer.rs     — Buffer (new, zeros, ones, from_slice, to, set_data)
6. hooks.rs      — HookedModule, register_forward_hook, register_forward_pre_hook, register_backward_hook, HookHandle::remove
7. rnn.rs        — LSTM, GRU, RNN + cell variants (LSTMCell, GRUCell, RNNCell)
8. rnn_utils.rs  — pack_padded_sequence, pad_packed_sequence, PackedSequence
9. lora.rs       — LoRALinear (forward, merge, parameters, state_dict)
10. qat.rs       — QatConfig, QuantizedModel, prepare_qat, ObserverType

Layer-2: scripts/regenerate_nn_structural_fixtures.py — Python script producing
  ferrotorch-nn/tests/conformance/fixtures_nn_structural.json.
  Encodes PyTorch-derived reference values for:
  - Sequential/ModuleList/ModuleDict nested forward chain
  - LSTM/GRU/RNN small sequence + hidden state multi-step trajectory
  - LoRA rank decomposition forward correctness + weight merge
  - QAT fake-quantize forward parity

Layer-3: ferrotorch-nn/tests/conformance_nn_structural.rs — test file covering
  all 10 modules. cascade_skip + crosslink-quick references for cascade bugs.

Approach:
- Create fixtures directory at ferrotorch-nn/tests/conformance/
- Generate Python fixture script (PyTorch torch 2.11 reference)
- Author the Rust conformance test file
- Run cargo test to verify clean
